topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the house had become a physical weight, pressing down on Liam’s chest. He was slumped on the worn sofa, scrolling aimlessly through his phone, the screen casting a pale, cold light on his face. His usual energy was completely drained; every movement felt labored. Mom had just asked him to help set the table, and he’d offered a mumbled, noncommittal grunt before retreating back into his self-imposed isolation. It was during one of these quiet, heavy moments that his mother, while tidying up the living room, noticed an unfamiliar icon tucked deep within his browser history. Curiosity, a rare flicker, nudged her to click, and a new profile popped up—one that seemed entirely separate from the curated, muted world Liam presented to everyone else.

[story 2]

I kept my gaze fixed on the chipped paint of the windowsill, the afternoon sun doing little to warm the chill that had settled deep in my bones. My shoulders were tight, hunched inward as if trying to physically shrink away from the room. I hadn't spoken a coherent sentence all day, just offered monosyllabic replies when my father tried to engage. Then, my sister, Maya, walked in, holding her own device, and a strange, almost imperceptible tension tightened my jaw. She didn't say anything, just showed me something—a private feed, a collection of images I’d never seen. A slow, confusing dread began to coil in my stomach, replacing the familiar dull ache.

[story 3]

The air in my bedroom felt thick and stagnant, mirroring the state of my mind. I was lying flat on my back, staring at the ceiling, the sound of traffic outside a dull, irritating hum. I hadn't moved in hours, the weight of unexpressed frustration making my limbs feel heavy and unresponsive. Suddenly, my phone buzzed—a notification from an app I didn't recognize. Hesitantly, I tapped it open. It wasn't a message or a school alert; it was an entirely different interface, a hidden corner of the internet. A slow, creeping realization dawned, and a strange, almost fearful curiosity started to pull at the edges of my inertia.
